reasoning: Provide a concise obituary-style summary of the given text, including key biographical details, death notice, and notable tributes.
output: John “Brad” Bradbury, the Coventry-born drummer who powered The Specials and later the reformed lineup The Special AKA, has died in England. He joined The Specials in 1979 and helped shape the band’s iconic Two Tone sound, contributing to hits such as Free Nelson Mandela. Bradbury took part in The Specials’ 2009 reunion tour and later led JB Allstars. The band announced his death, saying they were deeply regretful and requesting privacy for his family, with no cause of death disclosed. In a family statement, they praised his drumming as the powerhouse behind The Specials and a key part of the Two Tone legacy, noting his integral role in the 2008 reform and extensive touring up to the present day. The news comes three months after trombonist Rico Rodriguez’s death. The Specials, known for seven UK top 10 singles including Too Much Too Young and Ghost Town, had dissolved in 1981 but later regrouped without their former leading man. Fellow musicians, including Billy Bragg and Pete Waterman, paid tribute, highlighting Bradbury’s Coventry roots and pride in being in the band.
